## [03:01 - 06:02]

This is all still important except for the amount of time spent with them because that actually led to some false expectations I think. I'm not sure but I think. The client will never value our work to the true extent it deserves. We must work around this with what I refer to as the four pillars of value. Now guys the four pillars it represents a piece of value so to give you a few examples. Guys you can do this without actually having to do any real work. For me at this stage I was doing a lot of management, social media management and content as well. We have things like creative planner, brand report, general report, feedback form. There's four weeks of the month. Every week the client needs to receive a new piece of value. Some examples, a creative planner, a draft content, final content, a report, an IEG document so some sort of research or some paper. Something that I found that's of interest. A brand report tailored for them, a feedback form asking for some feedback from them or a long personalized email message etc etc. We're actually just keeping the client in the loop and letting the client know that from that first of the month to January to February we haven't just taken your money, click, go on the ad and then just come to collect it again on February 1st. There's that relationship being built and I still think that stands true to this day. You just don't need to make it say if they're asking how many hours a week did you work? That is one of the reasons I've gotten such a high referral rate is because I've built such incredible relationships with my clients.
